Multiple African countries whose ordinary passport holders qualify for VOA at reduced AU rate of NAD 1200: Benin, Burkina Faso, Burundi, Cabo Verde, Cameroon, Chad, Comoros, Cote d'Ivoire, Djibouti, Equatorial Guinea, Eritrea, Gabon, Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Liberia, Madagascar, Mauritania, Niger, Rwanda, Sao Tome and Principe, Sierra Leone, Togo, Tunisia, Uganda, Western Sahara Republic. Note: these countries' diplomatic/official/service passport holders also get full visa exemption (see conditional_access). Central African Republic ordinary passport holders were added to VOA list in September 2025 (see separate entry above).

e_visa.notes: E-visa available online via eservices.mhaiss.gov.na for all VOA-eligible nationalities; strongly recommended to apply in advance as it avoids additional NAD 400 manual service fee charged for visa applications submitted on arrival. Holiday visa (up to 3 months) available online.

visa_policy.notes: Effective April 1 2025, Namibia implemented new visa on arrival requirements for nationals of countries that have not reciprocated Namibia's visa exemptions. VOA fee: NAD 1600 for non-AU nationals; NAD 1200 for AU nationals. An additional NAD 400 manual service fee applies for applications submitted on arrival rather than online. E-visa can be applied in advance at eservices.mhaiss.gov.na. Visa-free entry (all passport types) allows up to 3 months per calendar year for tourism/holiday. Diplomatic and official/service passport holders from a wider list of countries are exempted. All visitors must have passport valid 6+ months, return ticket, and at least 3 blank pages. On September 23, 2025, MHAISS officially announced an additional 36 countries/territories added to VOA eligibility (Cabinet 19th session, September 2, 2025), covering small island states, Caribbean nations, additional European and Asian countries, and the territories of Åland Islands and American Samoa.

cbi.notes: No official Citizenship-by-Investment program exists in Namibia. The Namibian Citizenship Act 14 of 1990 does not provide for citizenship by investment. Citizenship is acquired by birth, descent, or naturalization (after lawful residence). No official government source was found announcing a CBI program.

programs.name: Investment Permit (Employment Permit for Foreign Investors)
programs.notes: Under the Foreign Investments Act 27 of 1990, a foreign national must invest foreign assets of more than N$3,950,000 into a Namibian business activity (or acquire >10% of shares in a Namibian company). This qualifies the investor for a 5-year renewable Employment/Investment Permit. After 7 continuous years with the investment retained, the investor may qualify for Permanent Residence Permit. Applications processed by the Immigration Selection Board (ISB) and Ministry of Home Affairs, Immigration, Safety and Security. Processing time approximately 8 weeks. Note: N$3,950,000 investment threshold is cited by private developers referencing the Foreign Investments Act; the mha.gov.na page does not state the amount directly.

programs.name: Digital Nomad Visa
programs.administrator: Namibia Investment Promotion and Development Board (NIPDB)
programs.notes: Introduced October 2022. Allows remote workers, freelancers, and self-employed professionals to live and work remotely in Namibia for 6 months (non-renewable; must wait 12 months before reapplying). Income requirement: min USD 2,000/month for applicant; USD 1,000/month for accompanying spouse; USD 500/month per child. Fee: NAD 3,300 payable upon approval. Cannot seek or take up local employment. Administered by NIPDB; applications via the Ministry of Home Affairs portal.

agreements: SADC (Southern African Development Community) - member state; no formal SADC Protocol on Free Movement of Persons in force as of data collection

validation_notes: CORRECTION 1 - Tajikistan reclassified: Moved from visa_free to visa_on_arrival. The previous record noted ambiguity ('may reflect updated policy'); the authoritative April 2025 Swiss Mission list (missionofnamibia.ch) places Tajikistan firmly in the VOA list, and multiple independent post-April-2025 sources confirm VOA status. The Embassy USA source listing it as visa-free predates the April 1 2025 policy change.

CORRECTION 2 - Uzbekistan reclassified: Moved from visa_free to visa_on_arrival. Same reasoning as Tajikistan - the April 1 2025 policy change placed Uzbekistan on the VOA list. Confirmed by the Swiss Mission source and multiple independent references post-April 2025.

CORRECTION 3 - Eswatini source updated: The source_url for Eswatini was previously namibia-botschaft.de (which lists it as 'Swaziland'); updated primary source to visitnamibia.com.na which uses the current name 'Eswatini'.

CORRECTION 4 - September 2025 VOA expansion added: 36 countries/territories added. MHAISS officially announced the expansion on September 23, 2025 (Cabinet 19th session, September 2, 2025). New VOA countries: Aland Islands, American Samoa, Andorra, Anguilla, Antigua and Barbuda, Aruba, Bahamas, Barbados, Bermuda, Bolivia, Brunei Darussalam, Central African Republic (ordinary passports), Cyprus, Dominican Republic, Georgia, Grenada, Haiti, Kiribati, Maldives, Malta, Monaco, Mongolia, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Palestine, Panama, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Samoa, Slovenia, Suriname, Sweden (already VOA from April 2025), Tonga, Trinidad and Tobago, Vanuatu. Primary source for original report: The Namibian newspaper (namibian.com.na); the MHAISS official list of 36 confirmed by Erickson Immigration Group (eiglaw.com) citing the September 23, 2025 MHAISS announcement.

CORRECTION 5 - Central African Republic dual classification: CAR ordinary passport holders added to VOA list (September 2025 expansion); conditional_access entry retained for CAR diplomatic/official/service passports as those still hold full visa exemption.

CORRECTION 6 - Digital Nomad Visa added to fast_track: This official program (introduced October 2022, administered by NIPDB) was absent from the previous record's fast_track section. Added with official NIPDB source URL. Fee NAD 3,300; min income USD 2,000/month; valid 6 months non-renewable.

CORRECTION 7 - NAD 400 manual service fee noted: An additional NAD 400 service fee is charged for visa applications submitted manually upon arrival vs online via eservices portal. Added to USA entry notes and e_visa section.

CORRECTION 8 - visitnamibia.com.na added to source_urls: This Namibia Tourism Board official source was checked and confirmed the visa-free country list (22 nations excluding Tajikistan/Uzbekistan, confirming reclassification).

CORRECTION 9 - Venezuela reclassification note removed: The prior record's notes on Venezuela's uncertain status have been resolved; Venezuela is confirmed VOA for ordinary passports and visa-free only for diplomatic/official/service passport holders.
CORRECTION 10 - nipdb.com added to official_domains: The Namibia Investment Promotion and Development Board domain hosts the Digital Nomad Visa program information.

VALIDATION 2026-06-27 - Re-verification pass: All visa-free (22 countries), April 2025 VOA (33 countries), and September 2025 VOA expansion entries re-checked against official sources. The April 2025 VOA list of 33 countries was independently confirmed by Embassy of Namibia in Sweden (embassyofnamibia.se) - exact 33-country list matches current record. Visa-free list of 22 countries confirmed by Visit Namibia (visitnamibia.com.na), Wikipedia, and Embassy Berlin. No new policy changes found for 2026.

CORRECTION 11 (2026-06-27) - Haiti fee exemption added: Official secondary reporting confirmed that Haiti ordinary passport holders are exempt from VOA fees under the principle of reciprocity, even though they are classified as VOA. Notes field updated to reflect this. Source: VisasNews September 2025 report corroborated by search results from the Namibia Embassy USA October 2025 PDF.

CORRECTION 12 (2026-06-27) - Embassy of Namibia in Sweden added to source_urls: This official government mission page (embassyofnamibia.se) independently confirms the exact April 2025 33-country VOA list.

CORRECTION 13 (2026-06-29) - Two territories added to VOA list: Aland Islands (ALA) and American Samoa (ASM) were missing from the September 2025 expansion entries. The MHAISS official September 23, 2025 announcement (reported by Erickson Immigration Group/eiglaw.com) lists 36 countries including these two territories. The Namibian newspaper article had referenced 35 countries but the official MHAISS count is 36. Both added to visa_on_arrival.

CORRECTION 14 (2026-06-29) - September 2025 expansion sources updated: All entries previously citing the Namibian newspaper (namibian.com.na) as source now cite eiglaw.com/Erickson Immigration Group, which reports directly from the MHAISS September 23, 2025 official announcement. The count is corrected from 35 to 36 throughout. Effective date corrected from 'approximately September 25' to 'September 23, 2025'.

VALIDATION 2026-06-29 - Re-verification pass: Visa-free list of 22 countries re-confirmed (Angola, Botswana, Brazil, Cuba, Eswatini, Hong Kong SAR, Indonesia, Jamaica, Kenya, Lesotho, Macau SAR, Malawi, Malaysia, Mauritius, Mozambique, Russia, Seychelles, Singapore, South Africa, Tanzania, Zambia, Zimbabwe) via Visit Namibia and Embassy Berlin. April 2025 VOA list (33 countries) re-confirmed via Embassy Sweden and Swiss Mission. September 2025 36-country expansion confirmed via MHAISS announcement. No new 2026 policy changes found.
gaps: The official MHAISS pages at mha.gov.na (countries-exempted, immigration-control, visas-immigration-control-act-act-no-7) were not accessible; data cross-verified from Embassy USA, Embassy Berlin, Swiss Mission, Embassy Sweden, Visit Namibia, and The Namibian newspaper.
The Namibia Visa Fact Sheet PDF (Version 4, May 2025) from mha.gov.na is image-based and contains no extractable text - unreadable by automated tools.
The eservices.mhaiss.gov.na portal returned SSL certificate errors in this validation session; complete current VOA country list from the e-services portal not directly verified.

The September 2025 expansion (36 countries/territories per official MHAISS September 23, 2025 announcement) is sourced from secondary reporting of the MHAISS announcement (eiglaw.com/Erickson Immigration Group) and The Namibian newspaper, not from mha.gov.na directly. The official MHAISS announcement date was September 23, 2025; the effective implementation date has not been confirmed directly from the government portal.
No official digital nomad or skilled worker fast-track extension or renewal pathway was found; the Digital Nomad Visa is explicitly non-renewable (must wait 12 months).
Dual citizenship policy under Namibian Citizenship Act not explicitly confirmed for naturalized citizens from official source - not recorded.

The RBI investment amount (N$3,950,000) is cited by private developers referencing the Foreign Investments Act; the official mha.gov.na immigration-control page did not load to confirm the amount directly.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS (type A - held third-country credential): No official Namibian government source found granting visa-free, VOA, e-visa, or eTA entry on the basis of holding a valid US visa, Schengen visa, UK visa, residence permit, Green Card, or any other third-country credential. All entry rules reviewed are purely nationality/passport-type based.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS (type B - special status / OCI): No official Namibian government source found granting entry waiver for OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholders or any equivalent diaspora/heritage status.

Some pre-April-2025 VOA countries sourced only from namibia-botschaft.de (Embassy Berlin) - e.g., Cambodia, Chile, Czech Republic, Hungary, Mexico, Nicaragua, Romania, Slovakia, South Korea, Thailand, UAE, Vietnam - have not been directly re-verified against the eservices portal due to access issues; embassy source treated as authoritative.

Haiti fee exemption confirmed by secondary sources only (VisasNews, search result summary referencing Embassy USA October 2025 PDF); the October 2025 Embassy USA PDF (namibiaembassyusa.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Namibia-Visa-On-Arrival.pdf) was not extractable as it is image-based.

visa_types.name: Visitors' Entry Permit (Holiday/Tourism Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term entry for holiday, vacation, visiting friends/relatives, or tourism purposes. Issued to nationals who are not visa-exempt.
visa_types.notes: Fee NAD 1,200 per visa; maximum 90 days per calendar year. Handling fee NAD 80 (non-refundable) payable on submission. Can be applied online via eservices.mhaiss.gov.na or on arrival (VOA). Applying online avoids an additional NAD 400 manual service fee charged at port of entry. Requires valid passport (6+ months), return ticket, and proof of sufficient funds.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to transit through Namibia en route to another destination.
visa_types.notes: Maximum validity 3 days (72 hours). Must be applied for in advance. Applicants must show proof of onward travel and valid visa for destination country if required.

visa_types.name: Visitors' Entry Permit (Business/Commercial Visit)
visa_types.purpose: Entry for business exploration, attending meetings, conferences, or workshops as a non-facilitator. Does not permit actual employment.
visa_types.notes: Falls under the Visitors' Entry Permit category alongside holiday/medical/family visit purposes. Fee NAD 1,200. Does not permit taking up employment; for work see Employment Visa or Employment Permit. Handling fee NAD 80 non-refundable.

visa_types.name: Visitors' Entry Permit (Medical Treatment)
visa_types.purpose: Entry for foreign nationals seeking medical treatment in Namibia.
visa_types.notes: Issued as a Visitors' Entry Permit for medical purposes. Requires documentation from a Namibian medical institution. Fee NAD 1,200; handling fee NAD 80.

visa_types.name: Employment Visa / Short Term Employment Permit
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes a foreign national to enter and work in Namibia on a short-term basis for up to 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Fee NAD 1,100. For employment beyond 90 days or 6 months, a full Employment Permit must be obtained instead. Requires employer sponsorship. Handling fee NAD 80 non-refundable. All applications for work authorization beyond 6 months must follow Employment Permit requirements (see Employment Permit below).

visa_types.name: Employment Permit
visa_types.purpose: Grants a foreign national the right to work in Namibia for a defined employer for up to one year, renewable annually.
visa_types.notes: Fee NAD 2,600 per year (for first application and extensions). Requires employer sponsorship, deed of surety, police clearance (Namibia and country of origin), proof that vacancy was advertised locally, medical and radiological reports, registration with applicable professional board, certified copies of qualifications, and two passport photographs. Processed by the Immigration Selection Board (ISB). Renewable annually.

visa_types.name: Student Permit (Study Permit)
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes a foreign national to reside in Namibia for the purpose of full-time study at an accredited institution.
visa_types.notes: Fee: NAD 1,600/year for primary/secondary school; NAD 2,200/year for tertiary education. Renewable per academic year. Requires proof of acceptance/registration with a Namibian institution, sponsor undertaking, proof of financial support, police clearance (for applicants 18+), medical and radiological reports, and two passport photographs.

visa_types.name: Permanent Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Grants a foreign national indefinite leave to remain in Namibia on a permanent basis.
visa_types.notes: Fee NAD 20,000. Requires police clearances from country of origin and Namibia, proof of financial resources, employment references, detailed CV, medical and radiological reports, and two passport photographs. Eligibility typically requires several years of prior lawful residence or qualifying investment. PRP holders may obtain a re-entry visa (NAD 800, valid 2 years) when travelling abroad.

visa_types.name: PRP Re-Entry Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows holders of a Namibian Permanent Residence Permit who travel abroad to re-enter Namibia.
visa_types.notes: Available to Permanent Residence Permit holders only. Fee NAD 800; validity 2 years. Issued alongside or in conjunction with the PRP.

visa_types.name: Temporary Residence Permit (Spouse/Dependent Children)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to the spouse and dependent children of a Namibian citizen or lawful permit holder to reside in Namibia temporarily.
visa_types.notes: Fee NAD 1,600 per year. Renewable annually. Issued to qualifying dependants (spouse and minor children) of Namibian nationals or permit holders. Full requirements must be confirmed with MHAISS directly.

visa_types.name: Digital Nomad Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows remote workers, freelancers, and self-employed professionals to live and work remotely for their overseas employer or own business while residing in Namibia.
visa_types.notes: Introduced October 2022. Administered by the Namibia Investment Promotion and Development Board (NIPDB). Minimum income requirement: USD 2,000/month for primary applicant; USD 1,000/month for accompanying spouse; USD 500/month per child. Fee NAD 3,300 payable upon approval. Non-renewable - must wait at least 12 months before reapplying. Cannot take up local employment. Applications submitted online via the Ministry of Home Affairs portal.

visa_free.name: Brazil
visa_free.note: Tourist visits only; business/official trips require visa

visa_on_arrival.name: Rwanda
visa_on_arrival.note: Entry visas issued at any entry point

outbound_official.notes: Source is the Embassy of the Republic of Namibia in Washington DC (namibiaembassyusa.org), an official Namibia government mission site, under the section 'VISA REQUIREMENTS FOR NAMIBIANS TRAVELING ABROAD'. Entries restricted to diplomatic/official/service passports only (Congo Brazzaville, Ethiopia, France, Germany, Ghana, India, Philippines, Switzerland, Turkey, Venezuela) are excluded from the ordinary-passport visa_free list. The mirco.gov.na embassy subdomains (Geneva, Berlin, Kuala Lumpur, Addis Ababa, London, Washington) all publish equivalent 'Namibian Nationals' pages but were unreachable (timeout/ECONNREFUSED) at time of research. No single domestic Namibia government page (mha.gov.na, mirco.gov.na homepage) dedicated solely to outbound travel for Namibia citizens was located; the Embassy USA page is the best available official origin-side source.
